title: Innodata Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Results
text: Innodata (NASDAQ: INOD) announced strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, with revenue reaching $90.1 million, representing 54% year-over-year growth and beating consensus expectations by 18%. The company reported net income of $14.9 million, or $0.46 per basic share. The company achieved adjusted gross margin of 47% and adjusted EBITDA of $25.0 million (28% of revenue), surpassing consensus by 139%. Cash and short-term investments totaled $117.4 million as of March 31, 2026, up $35.1 million from year-end 2025. The company's Wells Fargo credit facility was expanded to $50 million and remains undrawn. Innodata raised its full-year 2026 revenue growth guidance to approximately 40% or higher, up from the prior 35% guidance. The firm announced new engagements with a major technology company expected to generate approximately $51 million in 2026 revenue. Additionally, the company launched an Evaluation and Observability Platform in beta and secured its first platform engagement valued at $1 million. The organization transitioned to single-segment financial reporting, reflecting its strategic focus on agentic AI technologies. The stock surged 86% on the announcement day, with peak gains reaching 91.1% within 18 hours, adding approximately $1.38 billion to the company's market valuation.
